summary: Bug-fix verification for admin vs physician role separation. Backend: 11/11 new admin-role pytest cases PASS (role in /auth/me & login/register; 401 unauthed; 403 for physician on /api/admin/overview, /api/admin/users, /api/admin/users/{id}/grant-credits; overview & users list shape; grant-credits increments balance and persists; 404 on unknown user id). Frontend: admin login (dr.admin@pacopilot.health) correctly redirects to /admin, Platform overview + 7 stat cards + Accounts table (11 rows) render, sidebar shows 'Admin' item, grant-credits dialog works. Physician registered via /login register tab -> redirected to /dashboard, sidebar has NO 'Admin' item, direct navigation to /admin gets redirected to /dashboard. Bug ('admin and user see same screens') is fully resolved.
critical_code_review_comments: get_current_admin cleanly reuses get_current_user and raises 403 on role mismatch — good.
public_user() now consistently returns role for both password and google auth paths — good.
AdminDashboard uses data-testid on every stat and row action (admin-stat-*, admin-user-row-*, admin-grant-btn-*, admin-grant-amount, admin-grant-confirm) — well testable.
Grant-credits endpoint records a credit_transactions row with type='admin_grant' + granted_by (audit trail) — good.
Minor: DialogContent missing aria-describedby (console warning only, non-blocking a11y).
test_credentials: Admin: dr.admin@pacopilot.health / PaCopilot2026! (works, role=admin). Physicians registered via /api/auth/register with @pacopilot-tests.com domain (role=physician, 5 free credits).
seed_data_creation: Several physician test users created via /api/auth/register during pytest and Playwright runs (uitest_*, test_*@pacopilot-tests.com). No admin delete endpoint; cleanup not attempted.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Admin role separation bug is fixed and verified end-to-end. Prior iteration_1 blocker (EMERGENT_LLM_KEY budget) is unrelated to this fix and was intentionally out-of-scope per the review request.
rca of the issue: Reported bug (admin & user see same screens) was caused by absence of role field on user + no admin-only routes. Fix: added role='admin' on seeded admin (physician default for others), added get_current_admin dependency, added /api/admin/{overview,users,grant-credits}, added AdminRoute guard + /admin route + AdminDashboard page, and Login.js post-auth redirect switches on user.role. All layers verified.
